hero.title: Share a wallet. Spend together. Settle never.
hero.sub: Arkalyn-Kitty replaces circular IOUs with a shared wallet. Everyone chips in, every expense debits the wallet, and the receipts speak for themselves.

why.title: Splitting bills is a broken pattern.
why.sub: Every group expense app we tried treats the symptom — debt — instead of the cause. Arkalyn-Kitty removes the debt entirely by making the wallet shared.

points.title: IOUs don't scale past three people
points.body: Four roommates, six expenses, two reimbursements — and suddenly everyone's confused about who owes whom. A shared wallet collapses the math into one number.

points.title: Money disagreements are friendship taxes
points.body: Most splits start as 'I'll pay this one, you get the next.' Most end as silent resentment. A transparent ledger removes the guesswork.

points.title: Spreadsheets are not a financial system
points.body: A shared sheet is a workaround. Arkalyn-Kitty is purpose-built: contributions, expenses, roles, settlements — all auditable.

whoFor.title: Any group that shares a wallet.
whoFor.sub: If two or more people regularly spend out of a shared wallet, Arkalyn-Kitty is built for you.

audiences.title: Households & roommates
audiences.body: Rent, groceries, utility bills, the cleaner — chip in monthly and stop chasing each other on payday.

audiences.title: Trip groups
audiences.body: Goa, Manali, Europe — one pot for cabs, stays, food. Nobody floats the whole bill anymore.

audiences.title: Families
audiences.body: Joint expenses for parents, kids' school, festivals. A clean shared record without awkward Venmos.

audiences.title: Friend circles & clubs
audiences.body: Monthly dinners, gifts, weekend plans. Everyone chips in once, the wallet handles the rest.

audiences.title: Small teams & offsites
audiences.body: Team lunches, coffee runs, offsite expenses. Roles let an admin approve while members watch live.

audiences.title: Hostel & college groups
audiences.body: Mess money, project supplies, snack runs. No more chai-stall mental math.

items.q: How is this different from Splitwise?
items.a: Splitwise tracks who owes whom across many small debts. Arkalyn-Kitty avoids debt entirely — everyone contributes to a shared wallet, and expenses debit that wallet. No reconciliation needed.

items.q: What happens when someone leaves the group?
items.a: Members and admins can leave once their settlement is completed. The Super Admin can't leave — they either delete the group.

items.q: Can I edit an expense if I made a mistake?
items.a: No. Expenses are immutable on purpose — it keeps the ledger honest. You can delete an incorrect expense (with a reason) and create the right one.

items.q: Is my money actually stored in Arkalyn-Kitty?
items.a: No. We don't hold funds. The wallet is a ledger — you collect contributions however your group prefers (UPI, bank transfer, cash) and record them here.
Free to try while we're in early development. No card, no trial timer.

finalCTA.title: Stop chasing each other for money.
finalCTA.sub: Create your first group in under a minute.

howItWorks.title: Four steps from chaos to clarity.
howItWorks.sub: No spreadsheets. No reimbursements. No 'I'll pay you back next week.'

steps.title: Create a group
steps.summary: Add your roommates, trip mates, or team. Each person sets a starting contribution.
steps.detail: Name the group and invite members by email. Each member can set an optional starting contribution that's credited right away. The group gets a unique ID like Grp-26-001, and whoever creates it becomes the Super Admin with full control over roles and settings.

steps.title: Money goes into the wallet
steps.summary: Every contribution credits a shared balance. There's no who-paid-for-what to track — it's one wallet.
steps.detail: Contributions are recorded as credits against the shared wallet — collected by UPI, bank transfer, or cash, however your group settles up off-app. Every credit is logged in the audit trail with who added it and when, so the balance always traces back to its source.

steps.title: Spend from the wallet
steps.summary: Log expenses against a category. The group balance debits automatically. Receipts are immutable.
steps.detail: Record an expense, tag it to a category, and the wallet debits instantly — with an atomic balance check so the pool can never be overspent. Expenses are immutable by design: to correct one you delete it with a reason and recreate it, keeping the ledger honest.

steps.title: Settle when you're done
steps.summary: Closing a trip? Splitting up the flat? Run a settlement and the ledger closes cleanly.
steps.detail: When a member leaves or the group wraps up, run a settlement to reconcile their share of the remaining balance. Members and admins can only leave once they're settled; the Super Admin closes everything out by deleting the group.

features.title: Built for trust, not just tracking.

items.title: Shared wallet, not split-the-bill math
items.summary: One balance, one source of truth. No circular debts.
items.detail: Instead of tracking who owes whom across dozens of small IOUs, everyone contributes to a single pooled wallet and every expense debits it. There's nothing to reconcile — the balance is the truth.

items.title: Roles that mean something
items.summary: Super Admin, Admin, Member. Each level has clear permissions for expenses, members, and settlements.
items.detail: The Super Admin owns the group and can't leave without deleting it. Admins manage members, categories, and settlements. Members log expenses and contributions. Every route is permission-checked, so people can only do what their role allows.

items.title: Immutable expense log
items.summary: Expenses can't be edited — only deleted with a reason. Every change is traceable.
items.detail: There's no edit button on an expense, on purpose. Correcting a mistake means deleting the entry with a recorded reason and creating a fresh one. The result is a ledger that can't be quietly rewritten after the fact.

items.title: Per-group categories
items.summary: Food, rent, travel — categorise expenses your way and view reports by category.
items.detail: Each group defines its own categories, each with a custom colour. Every expense is tagged to one, so you can break spending down by category and see exactly where the wallet went.

items.title: Audit trail by default
items.summary: Every contribution, expense, and role change is logged in a group event feed.
items.detail: Every balance-changing action writes to two places — a transaction record and an admin event log. Nothing happens silently: contributions, expenses, deletions, and role changes all leave a permanent, timestamped trail.

items.title: Live group updates
items.summary: Socket-powered so balances, members, and expenses sync across devices in real time.
items.detail: The app uses Socket.io group rooms to push changes the moment they happen. When someone logs an expense or adds a member, every other member's screen updates instantly — no refresh needed.

closeGroup.title: Close Group
closeGroup.dangerDesc: Refund the remaining balance to members and lock the group. No further changes can be made after closing.
closeGroup.description: Closing distributes the remaining balance back to members as a single refund expense. Edit amounts if you need to override the proportional split — the total must match the current balance.
